findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of page 1 is present in the PDF but appears as a smaller/different rendering in the HTML version, though it is still present and recognizable.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a prominent state seal centered at the top; HTML shows the DEQ logo which incorporates the seal but in a different format.

findings.description: The HTML conversion places footer information (ROY COOPER Governor, MICHAEL S. REGAN Secretary, etc.) and header/department branding at the bottom of the page content as a separate block, whereas in the PDF these are positioned as a sidebar/header area at the top left.
findings.evidence: PDF: ROY COOPER Governor, MICHAEL S. REGAN Secretary, S. Daniel Smith Director are at the top-left of page 1. HTML: These appear at the bottom of the first page's content area, after the DEQ logo and address block.

findings.description: Page 2 of the HTML rendering duplicates the entire content of the letter. The scanned/image version of page 2 content appears first, followed by a re-rendered HTML text version of the same page 2 content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows the continuation of the letter once. HTML page 2 area shows the page 2 content rendered twice - once as part of the image-based rendering and again as parsed HTML text below it.

findings.description: The HTML has no h1 heading or any heading hierarchy at all. The document lacks any structured headings (h1-h6), with all content placed in plain paragraphs and divs.

findings.description: Page 1 content appears twice — once as an embedded image scan and once as transcribed HTML text. Page 2 similarly appears as both an image and transcribed text. The scanned page images duplicate all the textual content.

findings.description: Multiple nested <main> elements are used (outer <main> plus at least two inner <main> blocks), which is invalid HTML structure and misrepresents document structure.
findings.evidence: The outer <body> contains a <main>, inside which there are additional <main> elements for page 1 content and page 2 content.
